danger_signals.visual_form: Dark magical auras, undead creature appearances
danger_signals.meaning: Primary threat faction; evil sorceress Hecubah and her necromancer army represent existential danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Dynamically blacked-out portions of screen around player's line of sight
danger_signals.meaning: Hidden threats and unknown territory; danger lurks beyond visible area

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy creature spawns, combat engagement visual cues
Weapons and armor visual deterioration over use
danger_signals.meaning: Resource depletion and vulnerability; need for repair/replacement to maintain safety

safety_signals.visual_form: Non-player characters in safe locations offering services and story exposition
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones where traders, healers, and quest-givers provide support

safety_signals.visual_form: Fire Knights fortress Dün Mir, Castle Galava, Temple of Ix
safety_signals.meaning: Faction strongholds representing safe havens and power centers

safety_signals.visual_form: Conjurer healing spells, wizard self-healing, consumable items
safety_signals.meaning: Means to restore safety and health status

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC guide character providing narrative and quest direction
safety_signals.meaning: Allied support and guidance through dangerous world

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortress Dün Mir stronghold location; warrior-class associated
Castle Galava location; wizard-class associated; magical tower aesthetic
Temple of Ix location; conjurer-class associated; summoning magic aesthetic

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dark magical auras, undead creatures, necromantic visual effects
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hecubah's domain; necromancer lairs; dark/cursed locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Communicated through loading screen exposition and location backstory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs and factions act as remnants of this conflict; Staff of Oblivion disassembly created current political structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased necromancer activity, undead creatures, dark magical phenomena spreading across Land of Nox
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Factions mobilize; Jack recruited to gather Staff pieces; NPCs express urgency and danger

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three distinct strongholds (Dün Mir, Castle Galava, Temple of Ix) each controlling territory and staff piece
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must interact with non-chosen factions as temporary allies; each faction guards their staff piece through dungeon challenges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dungeon and wilderness exploration; enemy density and difficulty escalation; loading screens advancing narrative exposition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs temporarily follow Jack for assistance; quests advance toward staff collection and Hecubah confrontation

environmental_storytelling.example: Backstory of Jandor, Staff of Oblivion, Necromancer imprisonment in Orb, and faction staff-piece distribution revealed through location loading screens
Fire Knights fortress (Dün Mir), Wizards' tower (Castle Galava), Conjurers' temple (Temple of Ix) each represent faction identity and power base
Story advanced through character conversations, revealing world threats, faction relationships, and player objectives
Different enemy types inhabit different faction territories, reinforcing territorial control and thematic identity
Three staff pieces (Halberd of Horrendous, Heart of Nox, Weirdling Beast) serve as both gameplay objectives and world-state tokens of power

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Pulled from Earth into Land of Nox; disoriented; must learn world rules and factions
Gathers experience, collects items, assists NPCs, explores dungeons; temporarily commands allied NPCs
Equips heavy armor and melee weapons; recharging special abilities; cannot use magic
Summons monsters via Beast Scrolls; tames wild creatures; uses bows/crossbows; healing support
Casts diverse spell array; teleports; becomes invisible; limited armor capacity; high-risk/high-reward playstyle
Necromancer sorceress commanding undead army; primary threat forcing factions into temporary alliance with player
Provides guidance, narrative context, quest direction; represents established world power and defeated necromancer era
Assist in combat or require player escort to safety; cannot be controlled or re-equipped by player

notes: Nox communicates world-state primarily through faction stronghold locations (three-way power balance), narrative exposition via loading screens and NPC dialogue, and class-based playstyle differentiation that mirrors faction identity. The Staff of Oblivion's disassembly into three pieces serves as the central mechanic binding world-state (faction division) to gameplay progression. Danger is signaled through fog of war system (TrueSight), necromancer visual presence, and item degradation mechanics. The game uses location-based environmental storytelling rather than explicit visual HUD elements for world-state communication. Class choice at game start fundamentally alters which factions appear as temporary allies vs. challenges, creating branching political narratives.
